text: The video shows a patient undergoing a cholecystectomy after a sleeve gastrectomy. The sleeve gastrectomy appears healthy. The surgeon lifts the gallbladder, identifying the cystic duct and artery entering the gallbladder.
The surgeon uses robotic instruments to apply clips on the cystic duct, visible on the left side of the screen, and the cystic artery on the right side, sealing them in preparation for cutting.

The surgeon uses robotic scissors to cut the cystic artery, the blood supply to the gallbladder, and places an additional clip on the cystic duct before cutting it. The gallbladder is then separated from the liver bed using the robotic platform, providing a clear view of the anatomy.

The surgeon applies traction to the gallbladder, revealing the space to be freed from the liver. The robotic system facilitates the separation with minimal blood loss, using three 8mm instruments and a 3D high-definition camera for precise visualization.

The surgeon removes the gallbladder from the liver bed and places it to the side. The gallbladder is then extracted through a small incision at the belly button. The incision is closed with sutures to prevent hernia formation. The surgical field is cleared of any remaining air, showing the final result.
